items.title: Operator overloading: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? Operator overloading permits inventing new precedence or making + modify its left operand unexpectedly.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Overloading changes implementation, not grammar; preserve conventional operator semantics and keep invariant and equality rules explicit.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Overloading changes implementation, not grammar; preserve conventional operator semantics and keep invariant and equality rules explicit.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Overloading changes implementation, not grammar; preserve conventional operator semantics and keep invariant and equality rules explicit.

items.title: Operator overloading: executable application
items.prompt: Define Point operator+ to add corresponding coordinates without mutating either operand, read two points, and print the resulting coordinates.
